observations.title: Never use raw fs I/O on tasks.json, inbox.json, or activity-log.json
observations.narrative: Never use raw fs I/O (readFileSync/writeFileSync) or lowdb on tasks.json, inbox.json, or activity-log.json. Always use mutateTasks, mutateInbox, and mutateActivityLog from src/lib/data.ts — they hold an async-mutex lock. Daemon scripts (run-task.ts, run-task-comment.ts) must follow the same rule.

Daemon scripts run-task.ts and run-task-comment.ts must follow same rule

observations.title: The "me" agent is a built-in sentinel for human tasks
observations.narrative: The "me" agent (id: "me") is a built-in sentinel for human tasks. sync-commands skips it. Scheduler/dispatch refuse it. Hard-delete is blocked. Schemas default actor to "me". UI filters it from assignee dropdowns. Agent settings/editing must be disallowed for "me".
facts: UI filters it from assignee dropdowns

observations.title: LayoutShell owns activePanel state; activity-rail architecture
observations.narrative: LayoutShell (src/components/layout-shell.tsx) owns single activePanel: 'chat' | 'terminal' | null state. Old chatOpen/terminalOpen booleans and localStorage("mandio.chat-sidebar.open") are removed — do not re-introduce. Components: src/components/activity-rail.tsx (40px icon rail, always visible), src/components/right-panel.tsx (renders ChatSidebar or TerminalDrawer). Layout order left→right: [flex-1 main content][RightPanel ~480px][ActivityRail 40px]. Desktop: panel pushes content. Mobile: panel as Sheet side="right". ActivityRail renders unconditionally. RightPanel receives isMobile + onClose props.
facts: activePanel: 'chat' | 'terminal' | null — single state in LayoutShell
Layout order: main content → RightPanel (~480px) → ActivityRail (40px)

observations.title: Scheduled tasks use isScheduled flag in tasks.json
observations.narrative: Scheduled tasks live in tasks.json with isScheduled: true. Created via mutateTasks() in src/lib/scheduled-jobs.ts. UI filters them out with !t.isScheduled.

observations.title: Terminal architecture: xterm.js, WebSocket, PTY, and xterm gotchas
observations.narrative: Terminal components: src/components/terminal-drawer.tsx (content only, no Sheet wrapper — Sheet lives in RightPanel on mobile), src/hooks/use-terminal-ws.ts (WebSocket, ping/pong, resize debounce), src/server.ts (WS on same port, toggled by MANDIO_ENABLE_TERMINAL=false), src/lib/terminal/session-manager.ts (PTY), src/lib/terminal/upgrade-handler.ts (JWT gate). xterm.js gotchas: use container as state not ref; defer fit() via requestAnimationFrame; add SheetTitle with sr-only for Radix a11y. Terminal is rendered by RightPanel (activity-rail arch), toggled via mandio:terminal-toggle custom event.

Sheet lives in RightPanel on mobile
WS on same port, toggled by MANDIO_ENABLE_TERMINAL=false
xterm: use container as state not ref

observations.title: Workspace agents run Claude Code with CLAUDE.md and WIKI_PATH
observations.narrative: Task and conversation agents run Claude Code with cwd=workspace dir. Standing rules in workspace_dir/CLAUDE.md. Agents must read ai-context.md first; put docs/wiki/notes/specs under wiki (WIKI_PATH env var or ./wiki fallback). getWorkspaceEnv() in scripts/daemon/workspace-env.ts injects WIKI_PATH. Default agents: me, researcher, developer, marketer, business-analyst, tester, doc-maintainer. Self-healing lives in src/lib/data.ts ensureWorkspaceDir — create .bak before restoring missing agents.

observations.title: Wiki API surface, editor store, and wiki-link syntax
observations.narrative: UI entry: app/brain/page.tsx (not /documents). API surface under /api/wiki/*: GET/PUT /api/wiki/content (path query param for GET, {path,content} body for PUT), GET /api/wiki/slugs (consumed by stores/wiki-slugs-store.ts), POST /api/wiki/generate (spawns detached wiki-processor). Other routes: file, folder, init, latest-version, move, page, status, upload. Editor store at stores/editor-store.ts; caches last page in localStorage key kb-page-cache. Wiki-link syntax: [[slug]], [[slug|alias]], [[slug#anchor]].

observations.title: fix(cli): pin MANDIO_DATA_DIR in spawned server env
observations.narrative:
fix(cli): pin MANDIO_DATA_DIR in spawned server env
Next.js calls app.prepare() which loads .env.local via dotenv. Since
MANDIO_DATA_DIR was never explicitly set in the spawned env, any value
in .env.local would win over the ~/.mandio default.

Forward the CLI's already-resolved DATA_DIR to all three spawn sites
(startServerProcess, daemon-mode server fork, startMandioDaemon) so
dotenv cannot override it after the process starts.

Also document the chicken-and-egg constraint: MANDIO_DATA_DIR must be
a shell env var, not set inside ~/.mandio/env.

[6a3b188e] Anh Chu @ 2026-05-15T09:44:57Z

observations.title: fix(cli): set NODE_ENV=production when spawning server
observations.narrative:
fix(cli): set NODE_ENV=production when spawning server
mandio start was not passing NODE_ENV=production to the spawned Next.js
process, so the server ran in dev/turbopack mode even after a production
build. This caused on-demand compilation on first load, resulting in a
persistent login spinner until hydration completed.

Set NODE_ENV: "production" in both spawn sites: startServerProcess
(foreground) and the daemon-mode forkDaemon call.

observations.narrative:
perf: optimize chat conversation latency and streaming jank
Optimistic UI: user messages appear instantly on Enter before the POST
returns, with SSE dedup preventing duplicates when the real turn arrives.

conversations.json cache: mtime-based cache eliminates repeated file
parses (5x to 1x per continue request), keyed by absolute path to
avoid cross-workspace collisions.

React.memo: TurnBlock, ToolCallCard, and MarkdownContent wrapped with
memo. Markdown remarkPlugins and components hoisted to module scope for
stable references. Only changed turns re-render during streaming.

Daemon stream tail: fs.watch on parent directory with 50ms debounce
replaces 250ms polling. Falls back to 100ms polling if fs.watch fails.
Closure variable instead of globalThis for fallback interval.

Offset-based event reads: consumeNewEvents reads only new bytes from
last offset instead of the entire events.jsonl. Incomplete lines are
buffered across reads to handle partial writes.

Also adds 15 new tests across 4 files and records 4 critical hardening
items in TODO.md (SSE event loss, watcher drain, atomic writes, PATCH
whitelist).

observations.narrative:
Drop separate-process daemon, move autopilot into Next.js server
The daemon process was constantly failing to start or crashing. Instead of
babysitting a separate process with PID files and watchdog restarts, the
autopilot now runs directly in the Next.js server as an in-process cron poller.

What changed:

- Deleted 5 orchestration files (~2,200 lines): index.ts, scheduler.ts,
  dispatcher.ts, health.ts, recovery.ts
- Added autopilot poller in scheduled-jobs.ts with 60s tick + startup recovery
- Replaced daemon start/stop UI with a simple enable/disable automation toggle
- Added script-entrypoints.ts helper for dev/prod resolution (prefers dist/*.js,
  falls back to scripts/daemon/*.ts with tsx)
- Extracted wiki-processor.ts as a standalone script spawned by API route
- Emergency stop now marks runs as stopped in active-runs.json
- Renamed build-daemon.ts to build-scripts.ts, removed daemon:* npm scripts

No more is-the-daemon-running bugs. When the server is up, automation is up.

observations.narrative:
feat(planning): add project goal decomposition via Plan and Plan & Run
Closes the gap between 'project has a goal' and 'mission can dispatch'.
Previously, task decomposition required manually running /plan-feature
in a Claude Code terminal session. Now it's a first-class UI action.

New script run-plan-project.ts mirrors run-brain-dump-triage.ts:
reads project description + linked goals + active agents, builds a
decomposition prompt, spawns Claude to write tasks directly into
tasks.json with assignedTo, blockedBy, subtasks, and
acceptanceCriteria wired. --auto-run flag triggers the mission
immediately after planning completes.

New API route POST /api/projects/[id]/plan spawns the script as a
detached process. Guards against re-planning when eligible tasks
already exist (409).

UI: Sparkles (plan only) and Zap (plan & run) buttons appear on
project cards and the project detail page when no eligible tasks exist.
RunButton remains always present, disabled with tooltip when nothing
to dispatch.
